name: openspec-review
description: Validation gates only — run tests + spec validation + the 2-models-per-angle review panel + arbiter verdict on the CURRENT working tree against an OpenSpec change. No implementation. Use after interactive implementation (/opsx-apply), where compaction can eat in-session diligence.

verify.description: Shell command for the build/test check. Default suits pi-ext; override per project.

run: set -o pipefail; ({args.verify}); code=$?; echo; echo VERIFY_EXIT=$code; exit 0
git status --short && echo '---' && git diff HEAD | head -c 60000

phases.task:
Spec-compliance review of OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

For EVERY requirement and scenario in the delta specs under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/, verify there is implementation evidence (cite file:line). Also check every task in tasks.md is genuinely done, not just checked off.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If fully compliant, output exactly: No findings.

Spec-compliance review of OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

For EVERY requirement and scenario in the delta specs under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/, verify there is implementation evidence (cite file:line). Also check every task in tasks.md is genuinely done, not just checked off.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If fully compliant, output exactly: No findings.

KISS/YAGNI/DRY review of this change.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: over-engineering, needless abstractions or indirection, dead code, duplicated logic that existing code already provides, and scope creep beyond what the spec in openspec/changes/{args.change}/ asks for.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

KISS/YAGNI/DRY review of this change.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: over-engineering, needless abstractions or indirection, dead code, duplicated logic that existing code already provides, and scope creep beyond what the spec in openspec/changes/{args.change}/ asks for.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Security review of this change. Optimize for RECALL — surface anything suspicious, including cross-file and logic-level issues; a second reviewer will filter noise.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: command/shell injection, unsafe eval/exec, path traversal, secret or credential leaks, missing input validation, authorization gaps (IDOR), unsafe defaults, and data exposure.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Security review of this change. Optimize for PRECISION — report only findings you are confident are real and exploitable; classify each by OWASP category.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: command/shell injection, unsafe eval/exec, path traversal, secret or credential leaks, missing input validation, authorization gaps (IDOR), unsafe defaults, and data exposure.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.
